paneContent:
в”‚ cat .storyboard/terminals/${STORYBOARD_WIDGET_ID}.json | jq '.connectedWidgets'
  в”” 2 lines...

в—Џ read config via env file (shell)
  в”‚ ENV_FILE=$(ls -t .storyboard/terminals/*.env 2>/dev/null | head -1); source
  в”‚ "$ENV_FILE"; echo "WID=$STORYBOARD_WIDGET_ID"; cat
  в”‚ .storyboard/terminals/${STORYBOARD_WIDGET_ID}.json | jq '.connectedWidgets'
  в”” 18 lines...

в—Џ Read terminal-widget-plan--2026-04-18--08-35-33.png
  в”‚ assets/canvas/images/terminal-widget-plan--2026-04-18--08-35-33.png
  в”” 1 line read

в—Џ Yes вЂ” it's a screenshot of a previous Copilot planning session, asking clarifying
  questions about a terminal widget plan: architecture (ghostty-web + WebSocket PTY
  backend via the storyboard core server plugin), Copilot CLI integration options
  (A/B/C: general vs. purpose-built vs. both), and scope for v1.

вќЇ If I give you a task without ever mentioning the connected image, will you pull it
  into context?

в—Џ No. I only load connected widget content when your prompt references it (explicitly
  or implicitly, e.g. "implement this", "the connected image"). If your task makes no
  reference to it, I'll ignore it to save context вЂ” unless you tell me to always
  consider connected widgets.

 ~/workspace/storyboard-core/worktrees/0.5.0 [вЋ‡ 0.5.0*%]

вќЇ
